title: DCM Policy record
description: Machine-validatable Policy record (contracts/policy-contract.md; DCM ADR-006). Every Policy implements one base contract; what varies by policy_type is the `output` schema — what the policy PRODUCES when its match conditions are satisfied. UDLM carries the record; DCM's Policy Engine evaluates it (core-tenets G3). data-model-core.md §2 [D8.3].

record_type.description: Dispatch discriminator (data-model-core §2). record_type is the authoritative record kind; realized entities use resource_type, groups use group_class.

handle.description: Advisory human identity; uuid is authoritative.

tenant_uuid.description: Owning tenant (TEN-001; data-model-core §5 [D3]). A system-domain policy is owned by the platform tenant.

policy_type.description: The policy's kind — determines the `output` schema (policy-contract.md §8-17). 'gating' is the renamed 'gatekeeper' (2026-07 rename). validation/transformation/recovery/orchestration_flow/governance_matrix_rule/lifecycle are the contract's core set; placement (ADR-019) and itsm_action (§17) extend it.

policy_class.description: For validation policies: compliance-class (allow/deny, all must pass) vs structural-class (pass/fail field validation). Operational for others where the contract distinguishes.

concern_type.description: security | compliance | operational | recovery_posture | zero_trust_posture | data_authorization_boundary | orchestration_flow (policy-contract.md; open string — profile policy-group vocabularies extend it).

domain.description: Precedence scope. resource_type override wins over tenant wins over profile default (policy-contract domain precedence).

match.description: Match conditions (policy-contract.md §2, Model A field-conditions). A policy fires when these are satisfied.

field.description: A dot-path into the request/entity, or a known request attribute: data_classification | data_role | sovereignty_zone | tenant_uuid | resource_type | application_uuid | graph.has_cycle | graph.cycle_severity | graph.cycle_members | graph.cycle_mechanisms | graph.fault_domain | graph.blast_radius | graph.unmet_dependency | any spec dot-path (ADR-006 match sources; application_uuid is an allowed field). data_role (ADR-PROV-001) lets Governance-Matrix boundary policies control which roles a provider may receive at the DCM→Provider boundary — parallel to data_classification. The graph.* sources (docs/graph-integrity.md) expose DependencyCycle diagnostics so policy governs cyclic estates — e.g. graph.cycle_severity eq blocking / graph.unmet_dependency.severity eq blocking → deny; graph.blast_radius / graph.fault_domain for redundancy + impact policy (ADR-010); matched against the effective dependency graph, not a stored field. During the reserve-phase reconciliation loop (ADR-011), reserved facts are exposed as reserved.* match sources (e.g. reserved.segment_ref, reserved.<component>.<fact>) so a participating policy (see `reconciliation`) evaluates against holds as they land, before commit. Tenant-quota consumption is exposed as quota.* match sources (quota.dimension, quota.used, quota.limit, quota.exceeded — dcm-group.schema.json quota block) so policy governs over-quota at the commit barrier (e.g. quota.exceeded eq true -> deny).

lifecycle_scope.description: Which lifecycle operations the policy evaluates on (policy-contract §default lifecycle scope). Compliance-class validation + governance_matrix must include 'all' at standard/prod; fsi/sovereign cannot scope out any operation.

reconciliation.description: Participation in the reserve-phase reconciliation loop (ADR-011 §1; ADR-006 re-entrant evaluation; policy-contract §7.6). During reserve, holds land incrementally and each yields new reserved facts (a reserved segment, a placement). A PARTICIPATING policy is re-evaluated as those facts arrive, so placement / cycle / quota / governance-matrix rules act on the enriched reserved graph as it converges — not just once at the end. A non-participating policy is evaluated once, at the commit barrier (default). Default barrier-only keeps cheap static checks from re-running every iteration. Re-evaluation MUST be idempotent (ADR-006 soundness).

participates.description: true = re-evaluated on each qualifying reserve-phase change during reconciliation; false (default) = evaluated once at the commit barrier.

reevaluate_on.description: When participates=true, which reserve-phase changes re-trigger this policy. Omitted = all. A policy that only cares about newly reserved facts sets [fact_updated] to avoid needless re-runs.

output.description: What the policy produces when it fires (policy-contract §8-17). The required inner shape is conditional on policy_type (see allOf below). Additional per-type fields are permitted (extension surface).

audit_on.description: Which decisions produce audit records (e.g. ALLOW, DENY, STRIP_FIELD).
